children.label: i
children.text: represent the maximum practicable opportunity in each agency; and

children.label: ii
children.text: are consistent with improving agency management of the process for the review of collections of information established under section 3506(c); and

children.label: B
children.text: improve information resources management in ways that increase the productivity, efficiency and effectiveness of Federal programs, including service delivery to the public;

with selected agencies and non-Federal entities on a voluntary basis, conduct pilot projects to test alternative policies, practices, regulations, and procedures to fulfill the purposes of this subchapter, particularly with regard to minimizing the Federal information collection burden; and

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the objectives and the means by which the Federal Government shall apply information resources to improve agency and program performance;

children.label: i
children.text: reducing information burdens on the public, including reducing such burdens through the elimination of duplication and meeting shared data needs with shared resources;

children.label: ii
children.text: enhancing public access to and dissemination of, information, using electronic and other formats; and

children.label: iii
children.text: meeting the information technology needs of the Federal Government in accordance with the purposes of this subchapter; and

children.label: C
children.text: a description of progress in applying information resources management to improve agency performance and the accomplishment of missions.

children.label: b
children.text: For purposes of any pilot project conducted under subsection (a)(2), the Director may, after consultation with the agency head, waive the application of any administrative directive issued by an agency with which the project is conducted, including any directive requiring a collection of information, after giving timely notice to the public and the Congress regarding the need for such waiver.

children.heading: .—1So in original. Two subsecs. (c) have been enacted.1Inventory of Major Information Systems
children.label: c .—1So in original. Two subsecs. (c) have been enacted.1Inventory of Major Information Systems

children.text: The head of each agency shall develop and maintain an inventory of major information systems (including major national security systems) operated by or under the control of such agency.

The identification of information systems in an inventory under this subsection shall include an identification of the interfaces between each such system and all other systems or networks, including those not operated by or under the control of the agency.

children.label: A
children.text: updated at least annually;

children.label: B
children.text: made available to the Comptroller General; and

children.label: i
children.text: preparation and maintenance of the inventory of information resources under section 3506(b)(4);

children.label: ii
children.text: information technology planning, budgeting, acquisition, and management under section 3506(h), subtitle III of title 40, and related laws and guidance;

children.label: iii
children.text: monitoring, testing, and evaluation of information security controls under subchapter II;

children.label: iv
children.text: preparation of the index of major information systems required under , United States Code; andsection 552(g) of title 5

children.label: v
children.text: preparation of information system inventories required for records management under chapters 21, 29, 31, and 33.
The Director shall issue guidance for and oversee the implementation of the requirements of this subsection.
The head of each agency shall develop and maintain an inventory of the information systems (including national security systems) operated by or under the control of such agency;

The identification of information systems in an inventory under this subsection shall include an identification of the interfaces between each such system and all other systems or networks, including those not operated by or under the control of the agency;
